title: phyllanthus raivavensis
intro: Phyllanthus raivavensis, also known as the island sparkler, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in the Line Islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Phyllanthus raivavensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate liver and kidney issues, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water for its medicinal purposes, and its leaves can also be used to make a tincture or dried for later use.
